title: genius orchestration only thin turns
purpose: B3.1 defines genius orchestration only thin turns for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack.
scope: Owns `B3.1` only; siblings under `B3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §4 — Branch B — Cognition & routing plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#4-branch-b-cognition-routing-plane).
behavior_steps: [B1.1](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) route-tier and `model-policy.json` assign genius tier only for S3/S4 orchestration turns; implement-heavy work routes to economy workers when `spawn_workers` is true.
The conductor keeps each genius turn thin—spawn planning, merge, routing, and gate decisions—without implementing large task cards inline while `spawn_workers` remains true.
After S0 preflight, [B2.1](B2.1-conductor-genius-merge-route-platform-drain.md) reads `model_tier` from state.json and spawns workers rather than absorbing implement scope into the parent session.

Parallel spawn under orchestrate-subagents preserves worker budgets; the genius parent merges summaries via [A2.3](A2.3-post-step-route-tier-dual-write-increment.md) dual-write instead of re-implementing worker output.
If the conductor implements bulk code inline while `spawn_workers` is true, validate-workflow flags a tier violation and pursuit halts at H2 until routing resets to thin orchestration.

narrative: B3.1 defines genius orchestration only thin turns for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack. In practice, the system [b1.1](b1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) route-tier and `model-policy.json` assign genius tier only for s3/s4 orchestration turns; implement-heavy work routes to economy workers when `spawn_workers` is true, then the conductor keeps each genius turn thin—spawn planning, merge, routing, and gate decisions—without implementing large task cards inline while `spawn_workers` remains true.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-b 2026-06-28 -->

Genius-tier models are for orchestration turns only: routing, merge, gate decisions, and spawn planning—not for writing hundreds of lines of feature code. `model-policy.json` maps tiers; route-tier.py applies capability_class S0–S4 to pick worker vs parent model.

Thin genius turns keep cost predictable and preserve worker parallelism. If the conductor implements large task cards inline while spawn_workers is true, that violates this capability and collapses tier economics.
